perturbation.original_text: Moreover, the Recipient agrees to transmit the Confidential Information to only those Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of facilitating the Recipient’s discussion with the Company and who are informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Moreover, the Recipient agrees to transmit the Confidential Information to only those Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of facilitating the Recipient’s discussion with the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'who are informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and the terms of this Agreement', the contract no longer explicitly requires the Recipient to inform their Representatives about the confidentiality obligations. This contradicts the overall intent of the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction, as it undermines the confidentiality protections. Now, Recipient isn't required to let Representatives know of the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a condition to the Company’s disclosure of the Confidential Information to the Recipient, the Recipient hereby covenants and agrees that all Confidential Information will be kept confidential by the Recipient and will not, without the prior written consent of the Company, be disclosed by the Recipient, in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not be used by the Recipient, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than facilitating discussion with the Company. Moreover, the Recipient agrees to transmit the Confidential Information to only those Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of facilitating the Recipient’s discussion with the Company. The Recipient hereby acknowledges that it is aware, and the Recipient agrees that it will advise its Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement, that (i) the Confidential Information being furnished may contain or may itself be material, non-public information concerning the Company, and (ii) securities laws in the United States prohibit any person who has received material, non-public information concerning the Company or the matters which are the subject of this Agreement from purchasing or selling securities of the Company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities. The Recipient agrees to not engage, directly or indirectly, in transactions in Company securities while in possession of Confidential 2 Information, and further agrees that is will be responsible for its compliance with federal and state securities laws. The Recipient will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated that the Recipient inform their Representatives of the confidential nature of the information and the terms of the agreement. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text contains the original clause, imposing the duty to inform Representatives. The change creates a contradiction because the Recipient is now no longer explicitly obligated to inform their Representatives about confidentiality, which weakens the protection of confidential information and contradicts the original intent and explicit requirements of the contradicted text. This is an Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient hereby acknowledges that it is aware, and the Recipient agrees that it will advise its Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement, that (i) the Confidential Information being furnished may contain or may itself be material, non-public information concerning the Company, and (ii) securities laws in the United States prohibit any person who has received material, non-public information concerning the Company or the matters which are the subject of this Agreement from purchasing or selling securities of the Company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient hereby acknowledges that it is aware that (i) the Confidential Information being furnished may contain or may itself be material, non-public information concerning the Company, and (ii) securities laws in the United States prohibit any person who has received material, non-public information concerning the Company or the matters which are the subject of this Agreement from purchasing or selling securities of the Company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and the Recipient agrees that it will advise its Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement,', the Recipient is no longer explicitly obligated to inform its Representatives about potential insider trading issues. This contradicts the previous sentence establishing the Recipient's awareness of securities law implications, creating an in-text contradiction. The Recipient acknowledges awareness of the law, but the obligation to inform representatives is removed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a condition to the Company’s disclosure of the Confidential Information to the Recipient, the Recipient hereby covenants and agrees that all Confidential Information will be kept confidential by the Recipient and will not, without the prior written consent of the Company, be disclosed by the Recipient, in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not be used by the Recipient, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than facilitating discussion with the Company. Moreover, the Recipient agrees to transmit the Confidential Information to only those Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of facilitating the Recipient’s discussion with the Company. The Recipient hereby acknowledges that it is aware that (i) the Confidential Information being furnished may contain or may itself be material, non-public information concerning the Company, and (ii) securities laws in the United States prohibit any person who has received material, non-public information concerning the Company or the matters which are the subject of this Agreement from purchasing or selling securities of the Company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities. The Recipient agrees to not engage, directly or indirectly, in transactions in Company securities while in possession of Confidential 2 Information, and further agrees that is will be responsible for its compliance with federal and state securities laws. The Recipient will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the Recipient agrees to advise its Representatives regarding insider trading issues related to Confidential Information. The changed text removes this explicit obligation. The contradicted text highlights the Recipient's responsibility for its Representatives' compliance with the agreement and securities laws. By removing the Recipient's obligation to advise its Representatives, the changed text creates a contradiction with the Recipient's overall responsibility for the Representatives' actions regarding confidential information as stated in the contradicted text, and creating a situation where the contradicted text assumes that Representatives have been informed, whereas the change in the text removes the explicit obligation for them to be informed.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees to not engage, directly or indirectly, in transactions in Company securities while in possession of Confidential 2 Information, and further agrees that is will be responsible for its compliance with federal and state securities laws.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees to not engage, directly or indirectly, in transactions in Company securities while in possession of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and further agrees that is will be responsible for its compliance with federal and state securities laws', the contract creates a contradiction regarding the Recipient's broader responsibility for securities law compliance beyond simply avoiding insider trading. The remaining sentence still implies a general responsibility, but the specific commitment to compliance is removed, leading to ambiguity. The Recipient is only held liable for not using the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As of the date hereof and during the Term of the Agreement, JAM is and will remain in compliance with all applicable state and federal securities laws with regard to its investment in the Company and its rights and responsibilities under this Agreement, including but not limited to, the Securities Act of 1933, the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, and the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the Recipient agrees to not engage in transactions in Company securities while in possession of Confidential Information, *and further agrees that is will be responsible for its compliance with federal and state securities laws*. The changed text removes the latter portion of that sentence, thus removing the Recipient's responsibility for compliance with federal and state securities laws, now only holding them liable for not using confidential information. The contradicted text states that JAM (the Recipient) is and will remain in compliance with all applicable state and federal securities laws. Because the Changed Text now omits this specific requirement, it creates an omission that causes a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, as the Recipient's responsibility is significantly reduced in the Changed Text when compared to the original text, in contradiction with the contradicted text, and adheres to the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Designated Representatives ” shall mean the Chairman of the Board of Directors of the Company and persons designated by him.
perturbation.changed_text: Designated Representatives ” shall mean persons designated by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'the Chairman of the Board of Directors of the Company and' from the definition of "Designated Representatives," Section 2.6 loses clarity. Previously, only the Chairman, or someone they designated, could be contacted. Now, *any* person designated by the company can be contacted, contradicting the original intent of limiting contact to specific individuals. This creates an in-text contradiction because section 2.6 limits contact to Designated Representatives, but the changed definition removes a key constraint, impacting how the clause is applied. Previously this would be one person and or persons designated by them, now anyone by the company
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall have no discussion, correspondence or other contact with the Company or any of its employees, customers or suppliers concerning the Matter except with “ Designated Representatives ” of the Company. “ Designated Representatives ” shall mean persons designated by the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Designated Representatives' as the Chairman or persons designated *by him*. The changed text broadens this definition to anyone designated by the company. Section 2.6, represented in the contradicted text, restricts contact to 'Designated Representatives'. The change removes the limitation of designation by the Chairman, contradicting the original intent to limit contact to specific individuals approved by the Chairman. This leads to a strong in-text contradiction because section 2.6's controlled contact is undermined by the modified definition, potentially exposing the company to unauthorized contact. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type because essential language was removed that created a contradiction with obligations stated in another section.
